description_long: This triumphal arch is a compact, linear structure featuring five evenly spaced archways supported by robust, blocky piers. The arches are topped by a continuous horizontal entablature, which appears slightly weathered and textured, suggesting an aged stone or concrete construction. Each archway is identical in size and shape, with no visible decorative reliefs or sculptural embellishments, giving the structure a utilitarian and austere appearance. The piers are thick and rectangular, providing a sense of solidity and permanence. There is a small, raised rectangular element centered atop the entablature, possibly for structural or symbolic purposes. The overall coloration is a mottled brown, indicative of aged stone or concrete exposed to the elements.
description: A compact, five-arched triumphal structure with blocky piers and a weathered brown stone appearance, lacking decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five archways and the central raised element atop the entablature.
description_view_1: Angled view from the right, revealing the depth of the piers and the uniformity of the arches.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with all five arches visible and the central raised element apparent.
description_view_3: Angled view from the left, highlighting the thickness of the piers and the continuous entablature.
